title: TC001-Focus session can be started with a selected duration
description: Verifies a user can select a valid duration and start a deep work session with an active countdown.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Click the 45-minute duration button to set the session length.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click Start to begin the Deep Work session, wait to confirm the countdown decrements, then stop the session (click Reset).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click Start to begin the Deep Work session, wait and extract the status label and timer text to verify the countdown is running (timer should show Running and decrement), then click Reset to stop the session.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Running')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The session should show Running and the countdown timer should be active after starting a Deep Work session."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

Clicking Start did not start the countdown — the timer remained at 45:00 and the status stayed 'READY' after multiple attempts.

Observations:
- After selecting 45 minutes and clicking Start (two separate attempts), the UI still showed 'READY' and '45:00'.
- The timer did not decrement and no 'Running' status appeared.
- Reset was not reached because the session never entered a running state.

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Home quick action navigates to Focus module
description: Verifies a user can use a Home quick action to reach the Focus Shield page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Navigate to the Home page (/) then click the Home quick action that opens the Focus module to verify it lands on Focus Shield.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Reload the home page (/) and wait for the SPA to load so I can re-observe interactive elements, then click the Home quick action labeled 'Focus' to open Focus Shield.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Click the 'Start Deep Work' Home quick action (element index 623) to open the Focus Shield page and verify the user lands on Focus Shield.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[3]/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC006-Clarity engine decomposes a problem into prioritized task cards
description: Verifies submitting a problem produces task cards with priority labeling and context for urgency and impact.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Fill a concise problem description into the problem input area (textarea index 485) and submit the problem for decomposition (send Ctrl+Enter).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('I want a 6-month plan to become a backend developer: recommended learning path, key projects to build, technologies to focus on, and interview prep steps.')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Build a REST API')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The generated task cards should be visible after submitting the problem for decomposition"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Priority: High') and contains(., 'Urgency: High') and contains(., 'Impact: High')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The task cards should display priority labeling and urgency/impact context after decomposition"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

Submitting the problem did not produce task cards with priority labeling or urgency/impact context.

Observations:
- The page shows the problem textarea (index 485) with the submitted text, but no task cards appeared after submission.
- LLM calls timed out when attempting decomposition (previous submits reported timeouts).
- There is no interactive DECOMPOSE control present on the page to trigger decomposition.

        # Click element

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Create Your First Habit' button to open the New Habit creation dialog so a habit can be created.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the New Habit creation dialog by clicking the 'New Habit' button (index 487), then wait for the creation form/modal to appear.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div/button').nth(0)

title: TC008-Submit evening reflection and view Truth Report

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers

        # Click element

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Fill the three prompt textareas with reflective answers (start by entering text into textarea index 2).

        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[3]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('I completed 3 deep work sessions and finished the API integration I planned. I felt focused during those blocks and shipped the core functionality.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Instagram pulled me in for about 20 minutes after lunch and frequent notifications broke my concentration during the afternoon.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[3]/div[3]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Tomorrow I\'ll put my phone in another room during work blocks, schedule short breaks, and mute non-essential notifications.')
        
        # -> Click the 'Complete Reflection' button (index 219) to submit the reflection, then wait for the page to update and verify the Truth Report appears.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[4]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:

            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:

asyncio.run(run_test())

        if pw:

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC010-Clarity provides a single next action recommendation
description: Verifies requesting an immediate next action returns exactly one actionable suggestion.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Next action')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The page should display a single next action recommendation after requesting an immediate suggestion."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC011-Clarity tasks support step completion via checkbox interaction
description: Verifies a user can check off a step within a generated task and the step shows as completed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Fill a concise problem description into the problem input area (textarea index 137). Do not proceed to submit until the page reflects the input.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('I want to get a backend developer job but I don\'t know where to start.')
        
        # -> Click the DECOMPOSE button to submit the problem for decomposition (submit the filled textarea).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Completed')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The selected step should be shown as completed after checking it off."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
